summary:
The described pulpal configuration matches closely with what’s typically observed for a maxillary lateral incisor based on dental anatomy studies; thus it can be inferred this specific tooth belongs anatomically within such classification due primarily because its internal structure uniquely identifies itself through distinctive features aligned precisely along known patterns associated specifically only by laterals amongst all human upper front teeth variants considered here today.
The provided information strongly indicates we are dealing with either an immature or malformed permanent maxillary central incisor given common variations but more so leaning towards latter considering precise details about root shape & size not matching classic descriptions seen fully developed ones while still retaining some juvenile traits hinting at incomplete development process
